title: helichrysum sambiranense
intro: Helichrysum sambiranense is a flowering plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is commonly known as the 'Christmas Island itch grass' or 'Madagascar everlasting' and is prized for its medicinal and health benefits. The plant's essential oil, extracted from its flowers, contains sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, arthritis, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Helichrysum sambiranense include essential oils, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
